outcome.root_cause: Commit 1faea629b497d3250927292cc69072c8c20008be ("checkout: drop redundant session-cart null check"), the current live deploy on checkout-api, removed the optional-chaining null guard on getSessionCart(req) in src/routes/checkout.ts (line 16): `getSessionCart(req)?.items ?? bodyItems ?? []` became `getSessionCart(req).items ?? bodyItems ?? []`. getSessionCart() is documented and coded to return undefined for anonymous/no-session traffic. Without the guard, any checkout POST lacking a session cart throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at checkout.ts:12 instead of falling back to the request-body cart, producing the observed 500s and the error-rate spike (0.85 vs 0.2 threshold, baseline 0).

steps.content: Metrics confirm the breach (error_rate 0.85 vs baseline 0), and the log signature (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at `checkout.ts:12`) points straight at checkout.ts. The suspect deploy `1faea62` ("drop redundant session-cart null check") is also `is_current: true`. Let's verify the diff.

Confirmed root cause. `getSessionCart()` legitimately returns `undefined` for anonymous traffic (no session cart), and commit `1faea62` removed the `?.` optional-chaining null guard before `.items`, turning `getSessionCart(req)?.items` into `getSessionCart(req).items`. Any anonymous-session checkout request now throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at checkout.ts:12, matching the log signature exactly. This is a clean, high-confidence revert candidate — the diff is a single-line, well-understood regression.

tool_input.root_cause: Commit 1faea629b497d3250927292cc69072c8c20008be ("checkout: drop redundant session-cart null check") removed the optional-chaining guard on getSessionCart(req) in src/routes/checkout.ts, changing `getSessionCart(req)?.items ?? bodyItems ?? []` to `getSessionCart(req).items ?? bodyItems ?? []`. getSessionCart() legitimately returns undefined for anonymous/no-session traffic (per its own doc comment: "absent for anonymous traffic"), so any such checkout POST now throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at checkout.ts:12 instead of falling back to the request body cart. This is the deploy currently live (is_current:true) and directly explains the error-rate spike.
tool_input.title: Revert "checkout: drop redundant session-cart null check"
tool_input.body:
## Incident
inc_rec_bad_deploy_1783611348705 — checkout-api error_rate spike to 0.85 (threshold 0.2), first_error_at 1783611318705.

## Root cause
Commit 1faea62 removed the `?.` null guard on `getSessionCart(req)` in `src/routes/checkout.ts`:

```diff
- const items = getSessionCart(req)?.items ?? bodyItems ?? [];
+ const items = getSessionCart(req).items ?? bodyItems ?? [];
```

`getSessionCart()` is documented to return `undefined` for anonymous traffic (no session cart). Without the optional-chaining guard, any checkout request lacking a session cart now throws before it can fall back to `bodyItems`.

## Evidence
- `get_metrics(checkout-api)`: error_rate 0.85 vs baseline 0, over the incident window.
- `search_logs`: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at `src/routes/checkout.ts:12`, endpoint `/api/checkout`, status 500.
- `get_recent_deploys`: 1faea62 is the current live deploy (is_current: true).
- `get_deploy_diff(1faea62)`: single-line removal of the `?.` guard, confirmed against source via `read_file`.

## Fix
Revert 1faea62 to restore the null guard.

steps.content: PR opened successfully. Now closing out with structured findings.

[]` became `getSessionCart(req).items ?? bodyItems ?? []`. getSessionCart() is documented and coded to return undefined for anonymous/no-session traffic. Without the guard, any checkout POST lacking a session cart throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at checkout.ts:12 instead of falling back to the request-body cart, producing the observed 500s and the error-rate spike (0.85 vs 0.2 threshold, baseline 0).

Without the guard, any checkout POST lacking a session cart throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'items')` at checkout.ts:12 instead of falling back to the request-body cart, producing the observed 500s and the error-rate spike (0.85 vs 0.2 threshold, baseline 0).
